key_insight: MEDA is an LLM-guided, symbolic-regression agent that can discover biologically plausible ODE models by combining background knowledge, mechanistic constraints, and data fitting.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path toward automated model discovery for biological dynamics, especially when interpretability and structural correctness matter more than trajectory fit alone.
method: Retrieves domain knowledge to ground the search space and infer admissible state variables.
Generates mechanistic constraints and proposes candidate ODE structures with an agentic workflow.
Fits and evaluates candidate models, then compares retrieval, extrapolation, and open-ended discovery performance.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates MEDA on canonical model retrieval, reasoning-based extrapolation to unseen variants, and open-ended discovery, reporting strong structural recovery and biologically plausible models.
watch_item: A model can still match trajectories while producing biologically incorrect equations, so constraint quality and domain priors are critical.
paper_title: Automatic Ordinary Differential Equations Discovery For Biological Systems Using Large Language Model Powered Agentic System
